vessel: Crude tanker SOUNION (Greek flag, Delta Tankers)
summary: Laden with 150,000 t of crude, attacked west of Hodeidah by gunfire and three projectiles, then boarded and rigged with explosives days later; the burning tanker drifted for weeks before salvage, the year's closest call to a catastrophic spill. Position approximate.

vessel: Crude tanker BLUE LAGOON I (Panama flag)
summary: Struck by two ballistic missiles and a one-way attack drone while the SOUNION still burned nearby; the same day a second tanker was targeted off Yemen. Position approximate.
Hit by an unmanned surface vessel and missile strikes claimed as eight missiles, a drone and an unmanned boat; the port ballast tank was breached but the vessel kept steaming. Position approximate.

Attacked with unmanned surface vessels, missiles and small-arms fire; the crew abandoned ship and were rescued, and boarders later placed and detonated charges that sank her — the campaign's return after months of quiet. Position approximate.

Assaulted over two days with sea drones and rocket-propelled grenades, abandoned and sunk; at least four of the mostly Filipino crew were killed and others taken ashore — the deadliest attack of the Red Sea campaign. Position approximate.

source.name: Wikipedia · Attacks on the Eternity C

summary: Struck by a missile 128 NM east of Aden, well outside the declared area of operations; the nineteen crew were rescued by EUNAVFOR ASPIDES and one later died of his injuries. Position approximate.

vessel: Product tanker HELLAS APHRODITE (Malta flag, Greek-owned)
summary: Boarded by pirates using gunfire and RPGs roughly 560 NM off the Somali coast; the 24 crew held the citadel and EU naval forces freed the ship after some thirty hours — the loudest marker of the Somali piracy resurgence. Position approximate.

The IRGC announced the strait closed to vessels trading to or from the ports of the United States, Israel and their allies, a month into the air war; transit traffic, already collapsed, fell to a trickle. Position symbolic (strait centreline).
A skiff carrying armed men approached near Hodeidah and attempted to board; the skiff turned away after the master deployed a flare. Reported via UKMTO. Position approximate.

source.name: Wikipedia · Timeline of the Red Sea crisis

vessel: Container ship CMA CGM SAN ANTONIO
summary: The container ship was reportedly struck by a cruise missile while transiting the strait; eight crew members were injured. Position approximate.
The chemical tanker was attacked in the strait, the first Chinese-owned vessel targeted during the crisis. Position approximate.
Iranian forces seized the tanker, accusing her of trying to harm and disrupt oil exports. Position approximate.
The vessel exchanged fire with a small craft carrying six people near Balhaf, Yemen. Reported via UKMTO. Position approximate.

source.name: Wikipedia · Timeline of the Red Sea crisis

summary: The vessel reported an attack by armed men in a skiff approximately 30 NM southwest of Al Hudaydah, exchanging fire before the craft withdrew, as the Houthis announced a renewed ban on Israeli-linked ships. Position approximate.
Struck by a projectile in the strait; an engine-room fire forced the crew to evacuate with the vessel at risk of explosion. Position approximate.
The supertanker was struck by a projectile in the same wave of attacks and damaged; she was using the route recommended by the US Navy. Position approximate.
A third merchant ship was hit in the strait in the same exchange, hours before renewed US strikes against Iran threatened the interim ceasefire. Position approximate.
Attacked in the strait with damage to the engine room; the eleven crew abandoned ship, and one Indian crew member was confirmed dead on 14 July. Position approximate.
Attacked northeast of Qalhat, Oman — well south of the strait, on the coastal corridor shipping had been advised to hug — in the same day's wave of strikes. Position approximate.

vessel: Crude tanker MOMBASA B (Liberia flag, ADNOC-controlled)
summary: Struck by a cruise missile in the strait; one Indian crew member was killed and eight seafarers were injured across the two ADNOC-controlled tankers hit that day. Position approximate.

vessel: Crude tanker AL BAHYAH (Liberia flag, ADNOC-controlled)
summary: Attacked in the strait in the same wave as MOMBASA B; three crew members were declared missing. Position approximate.

Disabled by a US aircraft firing into her smokestack after ignoring repeated warnings while sailing unladen toward Kharg Island — the first enforcement action of the reinstated US naval blockade announced by CENTCOM on 15 July. Position approximate.
